commonPattern.invoiceType: Emporio Italia dense 4-column monetary table (Qtd | Preço Unit | Desc.(%) | Preço Total)
commonPattern.rowType: Weighted deli/cheese rows — fractional kg quantities, gross/net discount columns without % symbol
commonPattern.failureFamily: Emporio variance cluster A — Pass C GPT non-determinism on qty, unit_price, and/or line_total_net
mechanisms: discount_pct omission → gross used as unit_price (Prosciutto v23, Mortadella v28)
line_total_net synthesized as qty×unit instead of copying Preço Total VALOR

task7_instabilityOutsideGorgonzola.rationale: Historical instability on Bresaola (v27 40%), Mortadella (v28 0/3), Prosciutto (v23 non-deterministic €7–11). At v30 10-run: Prosciutto/Bresaola 100% stable vs PDF; Mortadella 90%; Gorgonzola 60%. Family shares same Pass C failure mode; Gorgonzola is worst but not unique.

finalVerdict.question: Should VL continue to Prosciutto or address extraction stability first?
finalVerdict.answer: Address extraction stability first — primarily Gorgonzola (v30 60% correct, v38 reread regressed to 2/9.35/18.72). Prosciutto/Bresaola/Mortadella are stable at v30 against visible PDF, but family-wide Pass C variance and fresh v38 Gorgonzola hallucination block confident VL progression.
evidence: Gorgonzola: only product with >1 distinct total on 4/10 v30 runs; totals range €13.44–€45.70
v38 live re-read worsened Gorgonzola from 1.05/10.88/13.44 to 2/9.35/18.72
Prosciutto v30: 10/10 identical 4.3/8.5/36.54 — safe to continue IF Gorgonzola fixed
Mortadella v30: 9/10 at 31.07; v28 had 0/3 correct
